operating_state: Total revenue increased 2% to $391 billion in FY2024 driven by 13% Services growth offsetting flat iPhone performance, with Q2 2025 accelerating to 5% growth led by iPhone Pro models and 12% Services expansion. Regional performance showed broad strength with double-digit growth in Europe, Japan and Americas while Greater China declined from iPhone weakness and currency headwinds.
cost_structure: Total gross margin expanded to 46.2% in FY2024 from 44.1% with Services margin reaching 73.9%, continuing to Q2 2025 at 47.1% overall with Services at 75.7% and Products compressed to 35.9% from mix shifts and currency impacts. Operating expenses increased from headcount expansion and infrastructure investments while component supply risks persist from single sources with custom components creating capacity constraints.
material_events: Kevan Parekh was appointed CFO effective January 1, 2025 succeeding Luca Maestri, while the company released €14.2 billion from escrow fully settling the State Aid Decision obligation. Apple announced $110 billion share repurchase authorization in May 2024 plus additional $100 billion program in Q2 2025, executing $95 billion in buybacks and dividends during FY2024. The company implemented Digital Markets Act compliance changes and launched iPhone 16 series with Apple Intelligence personal intelligence system.
macro_exposures: New U.S. tariffs targeting imports from China and other Asian manufacturing centers were announced in Q2 2025 with reciprocal responses, while DOJ semiconductor import investigation creates additional uncertainty. Foreign exchange headwinds particularly from renminbi and yen weakness adversely impacted international revenue performance given substantial manufacturing concentration in Asia.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued dividend increases with remaining authorization plus new $100 billion program, while maintaining focus on Services growth and Apple Intelligence rollout. The company signals continued R&D investment for competitive positioning through product innovation and supply chain diversification. Gross margins expected to face volatility from pricing pressures, competition, and tariff impacts while capital allocation emphasizes returning cash to shareholders.
uncertainty_profile: EU Digital Markets Act compliance remains contested with potential for significant fines and business model changes, while U.S. antitrust litigation alleging smartphone monopolization poses material business impact risk. Google search licensing arrangements face DOJ remedies that could eliminate revenue streams, with patent infringement litigations carrying injunctive relief and damages exposure. Ultimate tariff impact remains uncertain depending on additional measures and retaliatory responses.
